summary: Iter 4 CI fix verified locally. Backend health is green, the rewritten CI-aware run_regression_smoke.sh correctly reuses supervisor's uvicorn (no duplicate spawn) and runs 30/30 smoke tests in ~9s. Quick endpoint sanity passes.
action_items: Push the change and re-run the GitHub Actions 'Backend tests + coverage' workflow to confirm the spawn path works on a fresh CI runner (cannot be validated from preview).
critical_code_review_comments: Script is correct and idempotent: probes /api/health (3s timeout), reuses on success, otherwise spawns uvicorn with EXPORTed envs and waits up to 30s, with a trap-based cleanup of the spawned PID.
MONGO_URL default mongodb://localhost:27017 and DB_NAME=shelfsort_ci defaults are safe for CI; in dev, supervisor's running uvicorn is reused so these are not exported.
Minor: the `for i in {1..30}` loop's final-iteration error path runs after a `sleep 1` even on success of i=30 — fine in practice. No action needed.
Minor: `python -m uvicorn` invocation uses --workers 1 (good for deterministic CI). Log path test_reports/pytest/smoke_server.log will be helpful for CI debugging.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 4 CI fix verified in preview. /api/health=200, smoke band 30 passed in 9.15s via reuse path, only supervisor uvicorn process exists (no orphan spawn). /api/books returns 401 (auth-required, expected). /api/changelog returns 200. The CI 'spawn' code path itself can only be validated on the GitHub runner.
rca of the issue: Original failure: GitHub Actions runner only had MongoDB, no backend. Tests assumed http://localhost:8001 was up (true in dev container via supervisor). Fix: probe-then-spawn pattern in shell script means same script works in both environments. Verified the reuse branch in preview; the spawn branch must be validated by re-running the workflow.
